svg.text: Marks packet as requiring acknowledgement. Receiver must send back an ACK packet with the same seq_num.
Used for event packets (HIT, ITEM_PICKED_UP). Normal state packets are never reliable.
First packet sent by a client when joining the game session. Carries no payload.
Server uses this to register the player and begin accepting their state packets.
Acknowledgement packet. seq_num field echoes the seq_num of the RELIABLE packet being confirmed.
Sender stops retrying once ACK with matching seq_num is received. Payload is empty.
This packet carries the authoritative game state. Set only on server snapshot broadcasts.
Clients use this to identify world state updates and distinguish them from ACKs or other control packets.
Reliable event: a player hit another player. Always combined with RELIABLE (bit 0).
Payload: { target_id: u8[16], client_tick: uint32 } — sender is in header.player_id
Reliable event: a player picked up an item. Always combined with RELIABLE (bit 0).
Payload: { item_id: u8, client_tick: uint32 } — player is in header.player_id
Unused. Must be 0. Reserved for future packet types and flags.
player_count × PlayerState (variable size — depends on items_count per player)
structures packed back to back, no padding
World position of the player. Signed 32-bit to support large maps and negative coordinates.
Player velocity. Used by clients for dead-reckoning and interpolation between snapshots.
Movement / animation state enum: idle, run, jump, fall. Drives state machine on receiving client.
items_count says how many uint8 item entries follow immediately after. May be 0.
Because this is variable length, score always sits at offset +18 + items_count within the structure.
05 NORMAL PACKET PAYLOAD — client → server (unreliable, 17 bytes)
Godot float values truncated to int32 before packing. Server treats them as fixed-point integers.
Movement state enum value: idle, run, jump, fall. Server copies this into the snapshot unchanged.
